clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Provide evidence-based recommendations for managing hypertensive emergencies and severe hypertension in nonpregnant, nonstroke patients
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adult patients with BP >180 and/or >120 mm Hg with or without evidence of acute target organ damage
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: ICU admission determination, BP reduction targets, medication route selection, harm prevention

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point - assess BP and symptoms
Evaluate for acute target organ damage
Identify conditions like acute aortic syndrome/dissection
Determine need for intensive care admission
Select appropriate BP reduction protocol based on condition
Choose oral vs parenteral vs no additional therapy
Display BP monitoring timeline (1 hour, 2-6 hours, 24-48 hours)
Warn against inappropriate BP reduction when contraindicated

targetOrganDamage.description: Evidence of acute target organ damage

validationRules: Exclude pregnant patients - route to Section 11.5
Exclude acute stroke patients - route to Section 5.3.9
BP >180 and/or >120 defines hypertensive emergency when target organ damage present
BP >180/120 without target organ damage = severe hypertension

rules: Rule 1: If hypertensive emergency (BP >180 and/or >120 + target organ damage) → ICU admission for continuous monitoring + parenteral therapy
Rule 2: If hypertensive emergency + compelling condition (acute aortic syndrome/dissection) → Reduce SBP to <140 mm Hg (<120 mm Hg for aortic dissection) within first hour

Rule 3: If hypertensive emergency + NO compelling condition → Reduce SBP by no more than 25% within first hour (oral/parenteral), then to <160/100 mm Hg in 2-6 hours, then cautiously to 130-140 mm Hg in 24-48 hours
Rule 4: If severe hypertension (>180/120) + hospitalized for noncardiac condition + NO target organ damage → Do NOT use intermittent IV or oral antihypertensives to acutely reduce BP (Harm)

exclusionRouting.description: e.g., 'Route to Section 5.3.9 for stroke or 11.5 for pregnancy'

symptomsToAsk: Evidence of acute target organ damage (neurological deficits, chest pain, shortness of breath, renal impairment, visual changes)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Admit to ICU, start parenteral therapy, continuous monitoring
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Evaluate for severe hypertension without target organ damage - do NOT acutely reduce BP if hospitalized for noncardiac condition (COR 3: Harm)

appDisplay.screenType: Emergency alert with organ damage assessment
appDisplay.button: ICU admission protocol / Harm prevention warning
appDisplay.behavior: Rapid triage to appropriate BP reduction strategy or harm avoidance

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Follow BP reduction protocol based on presence of compelling condition and target organ damage
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor at 1 hour, 2-6 hours, 24-48 hours post-intervention
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Continuous BP monitoring in ICU; frequent checks during gradual reduction
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Immediate escalation for ICU admission or harm prevention alert for COR 3 recommendations
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP readings, target organ damage assessment, compelling conditions, and BP reduction protocol followed
